searchActions.result: Grep for banned comment labels in bm25-index.ts found no spec path, packet/phase number, ADR/REQ/TASK/CHK, or finding-id comment. The changed durable comment at bm25-index.ts:793-796 explains why last-batch finalization is duplicated without using ephemeral labels.

searchLedger.rationale: No shipped-code comment hygiene violation was reachable in the changed runtime file.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No banned ephemeral comment labels were present in changed shipped code.

searchActions.result: addDocumentFields removes an existing id first at bm25-index.ts:536-538, returns without indexing zero-token documents at bm25-index.ts:540-548, and clears scratch state in finally at bm25-index.ts:573-575. removePackedDocument decrements document length, document frequency, and postings at bm25-index.ts:935-958.

searchLedger.rationale: Empty/zero-token documents become absent rather than NaN-producing records; duplicate ids replace the old packed document and postings before adding the new version.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The reviewed degenerate cases do not produce a reachable crash, NaN, or stale posting from these code paths.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Chunk growth and width promotion for large postings

searchActions.result: Doc id chunks promote from Uint16 to Uint32 at bm25-index.ts:1074-1087, field term-frequency chunks promote from Uint8 to Uint16/Uint32 at bm25-index.ts:1089-1114, and searches iterate only `postings.length` at bm25-index.ts:1311-1323.

searchLedger.rationale: A term present in every document grows across chunks and promotes width before writes, while stale chunk capacity beyond length is ignored by scoring.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No reachable truncation or empty-result bug was found for posting growth within the implemented width bounds.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Packed field weights remain query-time tunable

searchActions.result: calculatePackedScore and searchPacked resolve fieldWeights per call at bm25-index.ts:1277 and bm25-index.ts:1302, and calculateWeightedPostingFrequency applies each field-specific weight at bm25-index.ts:1338-1348. The regression test passes custom flat weights at bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts:211-213 and expects the top result to change at bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts:218-220.

searchLedger.rationale: The packed path still reads per-query weights after finalization; packing stores per-field tfs separately and scoring multiplies them at query time.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No packed field-weight regression was found.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Runtime scope stayed on BM25 index and packed test

searchActions.result: The commit changed `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/search/bm25-index.ts`, `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts`, and phase-local spec metadata/docs under `017-bm25-warmup-churn-reduction`. No unrelated runtime file was touched by the target commit.

searchLedger.rationale: The implementation touched the packed layout, but spec.md:163 records the operator-sanctioned layout change if parity holds; scoring constants and BM25 term-score math remain unchanged in bm25-index.ts:48-92 and bm25-index.ts:1367-1370.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: No unrelated runtime scope drift or scoring-math edit was found.

title: Verification summary overclaims byte-identical ranking evidence
evidence: implementation-summary.md:91-92 claims `bm25-packed-inmemory` passed 6/6 with 134.3 MB and `hybrid-search.vitest.ts` passed 94/94 with byte-identical scores/order. The reviewed packed test has 6 tests and an unconditional RSS budget assertion, but ranking checks are `toBeCloseTo(..., 10)` at bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts:181-184 and metric/intent gates at bm25-packed-inmemory.vitest.ts:270-273. `hybrid-search.vitest.ts` was not changed by commit 573904538b and defaults to `SPECKIT_BM25_ENGINE = legacy-inmemory` in its beforeEach, so it is not evidence of packed score/order byte identity.
rationale: The code may still be correct, but the summary presents tolerance/metric regression coverage and an unrelated legacy-default hybrid suite as byte-identical packed ranking proof. That is a documentation overclaim against the phase requirement that ranking stay byte-identical.
